requirements.text: WHEN a request targets a protected backend API route without a credential, the system shall reject the request with HTTP 401 and a generic authentication error.
WHEN a request targets a protected backend API route with a valid admin token, the system shall process the request according to the existing route contract.
WHEN a request targets GET /api/projects without a credential before public sharing is implemented, the system shall reject the request with HTTP 401.
WHEN a request targets POST /api/projects/:id/crs without a credential, the system shall reject the request with HTTP 401.
WHEN a request targets GET /api/status or GET /api/health without a credential, the system shall return the health or status response without authentication.
WHEN backend API authentication configuration is absent in local development or test mode, the system shall preserve the existing no-auth request behavior.
WHEN a curl or server-to-server request has no Origin header, the system shall authenticate the request using the same credential rules as any other request.
WHEN MCP stdio transport starts without auth configuration, the system shall preserve existing stdio operation without requiring HTTP authentication settings.
WHEN MCP HTTP transport receives a request without a valid bearer token while HTTP auth is enabled, the system shall reject the request with HTTP 401.
WHEN MCP HTTP production Docker configuration enables HTTP transport, the system shall require bearer authentication by default when MCP_AUTH_TOKEN is configured.
WHEN an existing deployment starts without auth environment variables, the system shall continue functioning and emit an observable migration warning.
The system shall compare backend API tokens and MCP bearer tokens using length-checked timing-safe comparison.
The system shall add less than 5ms median latency per authenticated backend API request under the project test harness.
The system shall keep authentication enforcement centralized so protected backend API route coverage can be verified without per-controller drift.
The system shall not rely on Origin, Referer, or reverse-proxy identity headers as authentication credentials.
The system shall accept admin credentials from Authorization Bearer tokens and X-API-Key headers without logging raw credential values.
The system shall keep MDT-157 limited to authentication and shall not add public sharing, project visibility filtering, read-only policy, RBAC, TLS termination, or token rotation behavior.
The system shall expose unauthenticated health/status endpoints without exposing project metadata, filesystem paths, or credential configuration values.
IF a supplied token is empty, malformed, or a different byte length from the configured token, THEN the system shall reject it without raw token logging.
IF MCP HTTP auth is explicitly enabled without a configured MCP_AUTH_TOKEN, THEN the system shall fail startup or configuration validation with a generic missing-token error.
IF a reverse proxy does not forward Authorization or X-API-Key headers, THEN protected requests shall fail closed with HTTP 401 rather than bypassing authentication.
The system shall preserve all existing test behavior outside auth-enabled test contexts, and the existing test suite shall pass after MDT-157 changes.
The system shall document the no-auth migration path for existing deployments, including auth environment variables, production Docker behavior, and the meaning of migration warnings.
IF nginx or a reverse proxy forwards Authorization or X-API-Key headers unchanged, THEN protected backend requests with valid credentials shall authenticate successfully.
The system shall allow Vite dev-server frontend logging endpoints under /api/frontend/logs only from localhost or loopback clients because those endpoints bypass backend API authentication.

scenarios.title: Protected backend API routes reject anonymous requests
scenarios.when: I request GET /api/projects or POST /api/projects/:id/crs without a credential
scenarios.then: I receive HTTP 401 with a generic authentication error and no protected route data

scenarios.title: Valid backend admin credentials allow protected API use
scenarios.given: backend API authentication is enabled and I have the configured admin token
scenarios.when: I call a protected backend API route with the admin token
scenarios.then: the route responds according to its existing authenticated request contract

scenarios.title: Health and status endpoints stay unauthenticated
scenarios.when: I request GET /api/status or GET /api/health without a credential
scenarios.then: I receive the health or status response without an authentication challenge

scenarios.title: Missing local/test auth configuration preserves no-auth behavior
scenarios.given: the backend is running in local development or test mode without authentication configuration
scenarios.when: I call an existing backend API route without a credential
scenarios.then: I receive the same response that route provided before MDT-157 authentication was enabled

scenarios.title: No-Origin API clients follow the same token rules
scenarios.when: a curl or server-to-server client sends protected backend API requests with no Origin header
scenarios.then: requests without a valid credential are rejected and requests with a valid admin token are processed normally

scenarios.title: MCP stdio startup remains independent of HTTP auth
scenarios.given: MCP is started in stdio transport mode without authentication configuration
scenarios.then: existing stdio operation is available without requiring HTTP authentication settings

scenarios.title: MCP HTTP rejects requests without a valid bearer token
scenarios.when: I send an MCP HTTP request without a bearer token or with an invalid bearer token
scenarios.then: I receive HTTP 401 and the MCP tool request is not processed

scenarios.title: Production Docker MCP HTTP requires bearer auth when a token is configured
scenarios.given: production Docker configuration enables MCP HTTP transport and MCP_AUTH_TOKEN is configured
scenarios.when: I start the production Docker MCP HTTP service
scenarios.then: bearer authentication is required by default for MCP HTTP requests

scenarios.title: Existing no-auth deployments continue with migration warning
scenarios.given: an existing deployment starts without authentication environment variables
scenarios.when: the backend or MCP HTTP deployment starts
scenarios.then: it continues functioning and emits an observable migration warning with next-step guidance

obligations.title: Missing backend auth config in local development or test preserves existing no-auth behavior while non-local no-auth startup emits migration guidance
Backend admin credentials are parsed only from Authorization Bearer or X-API-Key and compared with length-checked timing-safe comparison without raw-token logging
Origin and reverse-proxy identity headers are never credentials; missing forwarded credential headers fail closed and forwarded Authorization or X-API-Key headers authenticate normally
MCP HTTP rejects missing or invalid bearer credentials when auth is enabled and keeps timing-safe token comparison and env validation locked by regression tests
Backend REST authentication is enforced once at the /api pipeline before protected routers, with health/status exemption classification before auth-disabled or credential decisions
GET /api/status and GET /api/health always next() before auth decisions and expose only minimal health data
Auth-enabled tests import the production-equivalent API test app factory, isolate configuration, preserve existing no-auth suites, and verify middleware overhead stays below 5ms median per request
Production Docker MCP HTTP defaults MCP_SECURITY_AUTH to true, fails clearly without MCP_AUTH_TOKEN, and preserves legacy no-auth migration warnings outside that default path
MCP stdio remains independent of HTTP auth settings and continues without auth configuration
Vite frontend logging endpoints are localhost-only and reject non-loopback callers before body parsing or state mutation

testPlans.title: Backend API auth config, credential parsing, route exemption, and timing-safe comparison
MCP HTTP bearer auth rejects missing, malformed, different-length, and equal-length invalid tokens
Existing server, MCP, frontend, and E2E suites remain green outside auth-enabled contexts
Backend protected route auth, migration warning, health bypass, no-Origin, proxy header, latency, and local no-auth contract
MCP HTTP env parsing, stdio transport selection, legacy no-auth warning, production Docker auth default, and missing token failure
Backend and MCP auth migration warning docs plus nginx credential header forwarding contract
Vite frontend logging endpoints accept loopback callers, reject non-loopback callers, ignore spoofed forwarded headers, and handle malformed JSON safely

tasks.title: Implement backend API auth module and unit contract
Wire backend auth middleware, health exemptions, and API contract tests
Set production Docker auth defaults and migration/proxy docs
Run latency and existing suite preservation gates
Harden MCP HTTP auth regression and stdio selection seams
UAT lock Vite frontend logging endpoints to localhost
